welcome.label: // vision-plugin
welcome.title:
Explore the plugin package
that powers the project view.
welcome.subtitle: This view shows the reusable Claude Code plugin itself: manifest, skills, commands, agents, hooks, bundled MCP, LSP, default settings, and scripts.
welcome.secondary: Switch back to my-project to see how a consuming repository enables this package without storing all plugin files in the app repo.

plugins.title: Plugin Core
plugins.description: The manifest, README, changelog, and license that define the package.

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: Reusable knowledge packs shipped with the plugin.

commands.title: Commands
commands.description: Markdown slash-command workflows bundled with the plugin.

agents.title: Agents
agents.description: Reusable subagents shipped by the plugin.

hooks.title: Hooks
hooks.description: Hook configuration and scripts bundled by the plugin.

mcp.title: Bundled MCP
mcp.description: MCP servers that start automatically when the plugin is enabled.

config.title: Plugin Defaults
config.description: Bundled LSP and default plugin settings.

children.name: plugin.json
children.label: Plugin Manifest
children.description: The manifest that defines the plugin identity, metadata, and discovery.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Frontend Patterns
children.description: Patterns for Next.js admin interfaces, tables, dashboards, forms, and performance.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Backend Patterns
children.description: Guidance for services, repositories, auth, queues, and structured logging.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Security Review
children.description: OWASP-style checks adapted for RBAC-heavy business applications.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: E2E Testing
children.description: Playwright guidance for multi-step workflows, RBAC, and import or export flows.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Deployment Patterns
children.description: Next.js deployment rules for Docker, CI, health checks, and rollback.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: API Design
children.description: Resource naming, pagination, filtering, versioning, and error response conventions.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Database Patterns
children.description: Safe migrations, indexing, RLS, and query design for data-heavy catalogs.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Git Workflow
children.description: Branch naming, protected branch policy, and PR rules for the plugin rollout.

children.name: review-pr.md
children.label: Review PR Command
children.description: A Markdown slash-command workflow for reviewing pull requests.

children.name: write-tests.md
children.label: Write Tests Command
children.description: A Markdown slash-command workflow focused on adding tests.

children.name: pr-orchestrator.md
children.label: PR Orchestrator
children.description: Lead reviewer that classifies a diff and delegates to specialist agents.

children.name: code-reviewer.md
children.label: Code Reviewer
children.description: Checks bugs, architecture fit, conventions, and risky implementation details.

children.name: security-reviewer.md
children.label: Security Reviewer
children.description: Focuses on RBAC, secrets, uploads, webhooks, and API route hardening.

children.name: performance-reviewer.md
children.label: Performance Reviewer
children.description: Looks for bundle regressions, data-loading waste, and expensive rendering patterns.

children.name: test-coverage.md
children.label: Test Coverage Reviewer
children.description: Flags missing unit, integration, and E2E coverage in changed areas.

children.name: architect.md
children.label: Architect
children.description: Helps design PIM, DAM, ERP, and e-commerce application architecture with explicit trade-offs.

children.name: hooks.json
children.label: Plugin Hook Configuration
children.description: Hook definitions shipped by the plugin.

tree.name: .mcp.json
tree.label: Bundled MCP Configuration
tree.description: Plugin-scoped MCP servers that start automatically when the plugin is enabled.

tree.name: .lsp.json
tree.label: Bundled LSP Configuration
tree.description: Language server definitions packaged with the plugin.

tree.name: settings.json
tree.label: Default Plugin Settings
tree.description: Default plugin settings applied when vision-plugin is enabled.

tree.name: README.md
tree.label: Plugin README
tree.description: The main overview of what the plugin ships and how teams adopt it.

children.name: branch-guard.sh
children.label: Branch Guard Script
children.description: Shell guard that denies dangerous git pushes from protected branches.

tree.name: CHANGELOG.md
tree.label: Plugin Changelog
tree.description: Tracks how the plugin evolves over time.

tree.name: LICENSE
tree.label: Plugin License
tree.description: Distribution policy for the plugin package.

terminal.welcomeTagline: Inspect the reusable package from its manifest down to skills, commands, agents, hooks, MCP, LSP, and default settings.

quickStart.description: see how Claude would review plugin changes

howToExplore: Read .claude-plugin/plugin.json for the package metadata and discovery model
Open skills/, commands/, and agents/ to inspect the reusable workflows shipped with the plugin
Inspect hooks/, .mcp.json, .lsp.json, and settings.json to see the bundled runtime defaults
Switch to my-project to see how a normal workspace consumes the package

doctorChecks.label: Commands
doctorChecks.detail: Markdown slash-command workflows bundled in commands/

doctorChecks.label: Agents
doctorChecks.detail: 6 agent files discovered inside the package

doctorChecks.label: Hooks
doctorChecks.detail: hook definitions and shell guard bundled in the package

doctorChecks.label: Bundled context
doctorChecks.detail: MCP, LSP, and default settings found at plugin root

initConventions: Reusable Claude automation is grouped into skills, commands, agents, hooks, MCP, and LSP
Consuming repos enable the plugin from their own settings without duplicating the whole package
memoryEntries: vision-plugin is a reusable package, not an application repo
Zeplin is bundled in the plugin through .mcp.json
Commands, skills, and agents are shared across consumer projects
settings.json and .lsp.json live at the plugin root as bundled defaults
